data.title: Ireland's prefabricated construction market to grow at 7.3% CAGR, reach EUR 1.61 billion by 2029 - ResearchAndMarkets.com.
data.description: In Dublin, a report titled "Ireland Prefabricated Construction Market Intelligence and Future Growth Dynamics Databook" has been recently included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. According to the report, the prefabricated construction market in Ireland is projected to see a yearly growth of 8.4%, with an expected market value of EUR 1.12 billion by 2025. This market segment in Ireland has shown consistent expansion over the years.

data.title: LKQ shares dive to 52-week low; Q2 EPS misses at 87 cents, sales at $3.64B (down 1.9% YoY); lowered guidance due to macro headwinds and lack of recovery in North American repairable claims.
data.description: LKQ Corporation saw a significant dip in shares by over 21% after reporting lower-than-expected second-quarter adjusted earnings and slashing its full-year guidance due to challenging macroeconomic conditions and a slow recovery in North American repairable claims. Their adjusted earnings per share for Q2 were 87 cents, missing the analyst estimate of 92 cents, with sales totaling $3.64 billion, a 1.9% decrease year over year. Despite a 9% decline in repairable claims industry-wide, the company's North American organic revenue performed better than the market average. In Europe, LKQ has revamped 25% of its leadership team, focusing on cost reduction, SKU rationalization, and revenue growth, including a strategic partnership to expand the salvage business.

data.title: GRS Technology Solutions attains CMMC Level 2 Certification with A-LIGN, bolstering federal cybersecurity compliance.
data.description: On July 24, 2025, GRS Technology Solutions, an IT, cybersecurity, and compliance provider focused on safeguarding federal contractors and their objectives, proudly revealed their attainment of the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC). This certification underscores GRS's commitment to ensuring the highest standards of cybersecurity for their clients.

data.title: FlowCore Water opens new location in Southlake, bringing 20+ years of experience in water solutions to North Texas.
data.description: A well-known water solutions provider, FlowCore Water, has recently brought its expertise of more than 20 years to assist communities in North Texas. The company, based in Southlake, announced the launch of its services in the area on July 24, 2025. Known for its comprehensive water solutions, FlowCore Water aims to cater to the needs of the region with its extensive industry experience.

data.title: Google parent Alphabet reported Q2 revenue of $96.43 billion, beating estimates, with earnings of $2.31 per share. Analysts are raising price targets after strong performance. Google Advertising revenue reached $71.34 billion, Google Search revenue at $54.19 billion, YouTube Advertising revenue at $9.8 billion, and Google Cloud revenue at $13.62 billion. KeyBanc Capital Markets raised price target from $215 to $230.
data.description: Alphabet, Inc. (GOOG) beat Wall Street expectations for the tenth consecutive quarter in Q2, reporting revenue of $96.43 billion, surpassing analysts' estimates of $93.72 billion. Earnings per share were $2.31, higher than the expected $2.16. Total revenue saw a 14% increase year-over-year with Google Advertising at $71.34 billion, Google Search at $54.19 billion, YouTube Advertising at $9.8 billion, and Google Cloud at $13.62 billion. KeyBanc Capital Markets analysts linked this growth to AI momentum and raised the price target on Alphabet shares from $215 to $230. Analysts are responding positively to the results.

data.title: FDA extends review period for GSK's blood cancer drug Blenrep combinations for relapsed multiple myeloma. New PDUFA action date is Oct. 23.
data.description: The US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has announced an extension of the review period for GSK plc's Biologics License Application (BLA) for Blenrep combinations for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma after one prior therapy. GSK's stock is currently trading within its 52-week range. The new Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) action date is set for Oct. 23, offering the FDA more time to examine additional data supporting the application. The BLA's efficacy is backed by results from the DREAMM-7 and DREAMM-8 phase 3 trials in this patient population. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Chainlink (LINK) surpasses $19 amid bullish momentum, up 19% daily to $18.52. Analysts target $22-$28 range.
data.description: The stock of LINK is currently priced at $18.52, showing a daily increase of 1.53%. It recently experienced a notable upsurge of 19%, surpassing the $19 resistance level. This growth is mainly attributed to increased institutional adoption, which is fueling optimistic market sentiment towards analyst targets ranging from $22 to $28.

data.title: Q2 success for Standard Premium shows improved efficiency and company growth, achieving impressive results.
data.description: The latest data reveals a significant increase in unemployment rates, with the national average rising to 6.9% in the past month. This surge represents a jump of 1.5 percentage points compared to the previous period. Furthermore, the number of jobless individuals in the country has reached 12.3 million, marking a substantial uptick of 2.7 million. The current situation reflects the ongoing challenges faced by the labor market amidst the economic uncertainty caused by the global pandemic.

data.title: Chinese VCs shift focus to crypto; Tian Tu Capital pivots to tech. Klook eyes US listing. Adaptation in changing market.
data.description: Smaller venture capital firms are making significant shifts in their investment strategies, with China Renaissance (1911.HK) venturing into cryptocurrency and Tian Tu Capital (1973.HK) moving from consumer goods to technology. Meanwhile, online travel agent Klook is planning a major IPO in the U.S. These moves reflect a broader trend of adaptation in a rapidly changing landscape. Despite crypto trading being technically illegal in China, Chinese venture capital firms are increasingly investing in cryptocurrency through Hong Kong platforms. The pivot towards technology by Tian Tu Capital highlights the changing dynamics of the consumer sector in China. The story indicates a shift towards more venture capital investments in cryptocurrency and technology sectors.

data.title: Bank of America sees potential for US banks to launch stablecoins due to the GENIUS Act, expects supply to grow $25-75 billion.
data.description: Bank of America believes the recently signed GENIUS Act will play a crucial role in regulating stablecoins in the U.S. This legislation, approved by President Donald Trump, is expected to boost infrastructure development and promote competition in the digital finance industry. According to reports, stablecoins—digital tokens supported by fiat currencies or commodities like gold—are projected to experience modest growth in the coming months. Bank of America forecasts their supply to rise by $25 billion to $75 billion due to new regulations. For the complete article, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Solana devs are working on roadmap to establish network as hub for internet capital markets. #crypto #blockchain
data.description: Top developers of Solana have come together to support a strategic plan with the goal of solidifying the platform as the leading hub for internet-based capital markets.

data.title: Loomis AB expands Pharma business by acquiring Kipfer-Logistik GmbH in Switzerland. 100% ownership deal.
data.description: In Stockholm, Sweden on July 24, 2025, it was announced that Loomis AB has made a significant business move by acquiring Kipfer-Logistik GmbH. The acquisition involves Loomis International Corporate AG purchasing the entire stake, totaling 100% ownership of Kipfer-Logistik. The acquired company, known for its expertise in logistics services, is based in Switzerland. This strategic acquisition further strengthens Loomis AB's global presence and capabilities in the logistics sector.

data.title: Versiv Composites and Bron Aerotech partner to supply Beta Cloth Fabric to customers worldwide for space applications.
data.description: In a recent update, Versiv Composites and Bron Aerotech, Inc. shared that they have commenced the shipping of Beta Cloth Fabric to global customers operating in space-related industries. The fabric is specifically designed for demanding applications in the space sector. This collaboration between Versiv Composites and Bron Aerotech will cater to the needs of clients worldwide, providing essential materials for space missions. The announcement, made on July 24, 2025, signifies a significant milestone for the companies involved and highlights their commitment to serving the space industry.

data.title: Altesa BioSciences to present Vapendavir data at ERS Congress 2025, showing COPD symptom improvement after Rhinovirus infection.
data.description: Altesa will be presenting data on Vapendavir at the upcoming ERS Late Breaker Session and at a symposium with prominent global COPD experts in Atlanta on July 24, 2025. Vapendavir has shown a consistent benefit in enhancing COPD symptoms following Rhinovirus infection, which is identified as the primary cause of COPD exacerbations. The findings highlight the significant potential of Vapendavir in addressing COPD-related issues, with experts convening to discuss its impact on patients.
